title: Demo News Briefing
subtitle: Three concise signals from a prepared editorial packet

cover.dek: A reusable HyperFrames broadcast built from structured input.
cover.caption: A quick scan of three prepared stories.

narration.text: Welcome to the demo briefing. First, a structured input file can now become a HyperFrames broadcast project. Second, each story carries a headline, summary, takeaway, optional source, and optional visual asset. Third, the workflow keeps rendering in HyperFrames so teams can inspect and refine the video package. Quick hits: the input format is reusable; the project writes a manifest; rendering can stay optional until the assets are ready. That is the briefing.

stories.title: Structured input becomes the source
stories.summary: The workflow starts from a small JSON contract instead of one local daily AI news folder.
stories.takeaway: Any prepared news domain can feed the same broadcast shape.
bullets: Title, summary, takeaway, and bullets are explicit.
Audio and visual files can be attached later.

stories.title: HyperFrames remains the render surface
stories.summary: The workflow writes an editable HyperFrames project with HTML as the source of truth.
stories.takeaway: The team can preview, inspect, and render through the normal HyperFrames CLI.
bullets: index.html, DESIGN.md, storyboard.json, and script.txt are generated.

stories.title: The workflow fits shotfun-agent
stories.summary: The run writes manifests, step sidecars, and userArtifacts like the other workflows.
stories.takeaway: Colleagues can reuse the workflow without copying a private local skill.
bullets: ShotFun TTS can be enabled when needed.

quickHits.label: HF
quickHits.text: HyperFrames is still the actual video tool.

quickHits.label: TEAM
quickHits.text: The workflow can live under shotfun-agent.
